mnemonics: PAN Loves Very Personal Connections (for PAN, LAN, MAN, WAN)
keyPoints: PAN (Personal Area Network) is for very short-range, personal device connections (e.g., Bluetooth).
LAN (Local Area Network) covers a small, localized area like a home, office, or building.
WAN (Wide Area Network) spans a large geographical area, such as a country or the internet itself.
commonMistakes: Confusing a MAN (Metropolitan Area Network) with a large LAN; a MAN is a network for a city, while a LAN is for a single building or campus.
Assuming a WAN is just a bigger LAN; WANs use different technologies and protocols (like MPLS or leased lines) to connect over long distances.
